Overview of Changes
The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) is currently revising 41 standards for grades of fresh and processed fruits and vegetables. One significant change is the removal of the "Unclassified" category from each standard. This revision aims to better reflect contemporary marketing practices and provide the industry with greater flexibility in how products are classified and marketed. USDA
Purpose of the Revisions
Alignment with Current Practices: The updates are designed to ensure that the standards accurately represent today's marketing practices, which have evolved significantly since the original 1959 standards were established.
Quality Assurance: The revisions will help maintain quality assurance in the industry, providing a common language for trading and ensuring that products meet specific quality levels. 1
Support for Industry: The changes are intended to support the fruit, vegetable, nut, and specialty crop industries by resolving disputes over product quality and promoting efficiency in marketing and procurement. 1
